title: Electrochemical liquid treatment apparatus
abstract: The present invention relates to an electrochemical liquid treatment apparatus and to methods of electrochemically treating a liquid. The apparatus may include: a treatment chamber containing at least one inlet for entry of a liquid to be treated, and including at least one outlet for exit of electrochemically treated liquid; a plurality of electrodes positioned within the treatment chamber for electrochemical treatment of the liquid; and a flow aligner for aligning the flow of the liquid between the electrodes, wherein the flow aligner is positioned intermediate the at least one inlet and the electrodes.
claims: Claims 1. An electrochemical liquid treatment apparatus including: a treatment chamber containing at least one inlet for entry of a liquid to be treated, and including at least one outlet for exit of electrochemically treated liquid; a plurality of electrodes positioned within the treatment chamber for electrochemical treatment of the liquid; and a flow aligner for aligning the flow of the liquid between the electrodes, wherein the flow aligner is positioned intermediate the at least one inlet and the electrodes. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the flow aligner is for distributing the liquid between the electrodes. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the flow aligner is a wall defining a plurality of apertures for passage of the liquid. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the wall is configured to provide a greater liquid pressure on the side of the wall proximate to the at least one inlet than on the side of the wall proximate to the electrodes when the apparatus is in use. 5. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the apparatus includes at least one electrode holder for holding a plurality of electrodes, wherein the electrode holder is configured to releasably engage with the treatment chamber. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the at least one electrode holder includes the flow aligner. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the apparatus includes a plurality of electrode holders and each said electrode holder includes a segment of the flow aligner. 8. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the treatment chamber includes a liquid disperser for dispersing the liquid to be treated into the treatment chamber, wherein said liquid disperser includes a plurality of inlets within the treatment chamber for entry of a liquid to be treated. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the liquid disperser is a tube perforated along its length to provide said plurality of inlets. 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the treatment chamber has a first wall and one or more side walls, wherein the first wall is distal to the electrodes and proximate to the at least one inlet, and wherein the first wall has an inner surface configured to direct the flow of liquid towards the electrodes. 11. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the inner surface of the first wall includes at least one channel which narrows to its base. 12. The apparatus of claim 11, wherein the treatment chamber includes at least one liquid disperser for dispersing the liquid to be treated into the treatment chamber, wherein said liquid disperser includes a plurality of inlets within the treatment chamber for entry of a liquid to be treated, and wherein within each said at least one channel is positioned one said liquid disperser. 13. An electrochemical liquid treatment apparatus, the apparatus including a treatment chamber containing at least one inlet for entry of a liquid to be treated, and including at least one outlet for exit of electrochemically treated liquid, wherein the treatment chamber is configured to releasably engage with at least one electrode holder holding a plurality of electrodes for electrolytic treatment of the liquid, wherein said electrode holder includes a flow aligner for aligning the flow of the liquid between the electrodes, and wherein when the electrode holder is releasably engaged with the treatment chamber said flow aligner is positioned intermediate the at least one inlet and the electrodes. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the flow aligner is a wall defining a plurality of apertures for passage of the liquid, and wherein the treatment chamber includes a shelf upon which the electrode holder rests when the electrode holder is releasably engaged with the treatment chamber. 15. An electrode holder for an electrochemical liquid treatment apparatus, the electrode holder holding a plurality of electrodes and being configured to releasably engage with a treatment chamber of the electrochemical liquid treatment apparatus, wherein said electrode holder includes a flow aligner for aligning the flow of the liquid between the electrodes. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the flow aligner is for distributing the liquid between the electrodes. 17. The apparatus of claim 15 or 16, wherein the flow aligner is a wall defining a plurality of apertures for passage of the liquid. 18. A method of electrochemically treating a liquid, the method including the steps of: - introducing a liquid to be treated into the apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 12; - applying a voltage to at least two of said plurality of electrodes to provide at least one cathode and at least one anode to thereby electrochemically treat the liquid; and - removing electrochemically treated liquid from the apparatus. 19. The method of claim 18, further including the step of introducing a treatment agent into the apparatus.
